---
title: "Data.rmd"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-30"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
#https://api.eia.gov/v2/electricity/retail-sales/data/?frequency=monthly&data[0]=price&data[1]=sales&start=2020-01&end=2025-12&sort[0][column]=period&sort[0][direction]=desc&offset=0&length=5000

#needed cause trying to download everything at once doesn't work, so we have to split it up into multiple files.
#for (year in 2018:2025) {
  #linkTemplate = "https://api.eia.gov/v2/electricity/retail-sales/data/?frequency=monthly&data[0]=price&data[1]=sales&sort[0][column]=period&sort[0][direction]=desc&offset=0&length=5000&api_key=gZv331F0xrjrk5bsB2nw0Y4ldiBDmnsTJXYUSMrY";
  
  #downloadString = paste0(linkTemplate, "&start=", year, "-01", "&end=", year, "-12");
  #download.file(downloadString, paste0("electricity_data_", year, ".json"));
#}

#all we need is period, stateid (filter out entries that are not "ALL"), price, sales, price-units, sales-units

#parse each JSON, write data back as one .csv
files = list.files(pattern = "\\.json$");
files;

library(jsonlite);
library(dplyr);


#test = fromJSON(files[1], flatten = TRUE)
#str(test)

#this is weird lambda syntax for combining all data files
data = files %>% 
  lapply(function(f) {
    json = fromJSON(f, flatten = TRUE)
    json$response$data
  }) %>%
  bind_rows();

#data;

#fix NAs, convert char of price to float
relevantData = data %>% 
  select(period, stateid, sectorid, price, sales, "price-units", "sales-units") %>%
  filter(sectorid == "ALL", !is.na(price), !is.na(sales)) %>%
  mutate(price = as.numeric(price));

relevantData;

#2 of our columns seem to be useless. Confirming that they are all the same value
print(nrow(relevantData));
tempPriceUnit = relevantData %>% filter(`price-units` == "cents per kilowatt-hour");
print(nrow(tempPriceUnit));
tempSalesUnit = relevantData %>% filter(`sales-units` == "million kilowatt hours");
print(nrow(tempSalesUnit));

#all 3 print 5952, which means we can always assume that price-units is "cents per kilowatt-hour" and sales-units is "million kilowatt hours" 

leanData = relevantData %>% select(period, stateid, price, sales) %>% filter(nchar(stateid) == 2);
leanData;



#write.csv(leanData,"CleanedData.csv", row.names = FALSE);

#only the most recent data, Dec 2025, and filter out WashingtonDC and the entire US
leanDec2025Data = leanData %>% filter(startsWith(period, "2025-12") & (stateid != "DC") & (stateid != "US"));
leanDec2025Data;

#write.csv(leanDec2025Data,"RecentCleanedData.csv", row.names = FALSE);

priceDifferenceDataStart = leanData %>% filter(startsWith(period, "2020-01") & (stateid != "DC") & (stateid != "US"));

priceDifferenceDataEnd = leanData %>% filter(startsWith(period, "2025-12") & (stateid != "DC") & (stateid != "US"));
#names(priceDifferenceDataEnd)[names(priceDifferenceDataEnd) == "price"] = "price_difference";
#totalPriceDifference = priceDifferenceDataStart %>% 

#rename both columns to price_difference
priceDifferenceDataStart <- leanData %>%
  filter(startsWith(period, "2022-01") & (stateid != "DC") & (stateid != "US")) %>%
  select(stateid, price) %>%
  rename(price_difference = price);

priceDifferenceDataEnd = leanData %>%
  filter(startsWith(period, "2025-12") & (stateid != "DC") & (stateid != "US"));

#join on state, mutate difference to be end / start
totalPriceDifference = priceDifferenceDataStart %>%
  full_join(priceDifferenceDataEnd, by="stateid") %>%
  mutate(price_difference = price - price_difference);

priceDifferenceMinimal = totalPriceDifference %>% select(stateid, price_difference);
write.csv(priceDifferenceMinimal,"PriceDifference.csv", row.names = FALSE);
```
